title: Polish expiration copy and make paste creation progress immediate

requirements.summary: Remove redundant “left” wording from the paste expiration label so it reads naturally.
acceptanceCriteria: On PasteViewPage, the expiration label does not contain the word “left”.
Expiration label examples render as “Expires in 9 min”, “Expires in 1 hr 10 min”, “Expires in 2 days 5 hrs” (no trailing/duplicate wording).
No other UI text regresses into awkward phrasing such as “Expires in Less than 1 min left”.

file_operations.description: Update remaining-time formatting to return strings without trailing “left” (e.g., “9 min”, “1 hr 10 min”, “2 days 5 hrs”, “Less than 1 min”), keeping pluralization correct.
Ensure the expiration label composes cleanly as “Expires in {formatRemainingTime(...) }” with no duplicate wording after the formatter change.

requirements.summary: Show an in-form status/progress panel immediately on “Create Paste” click, including a clear pre-upload preparation state.
acceptanceCriteria: When the user clicks “Create Paste”, an in-form status/progress panel becomes visible immediately (within the same tick/frame) and stays visible through upload + final paste creation.
If file reading/preparation time occurs before upload progress callbacks start, the UI shows a clear intermediate status (e.g., “Preparing files…” or equivalent) instead of appearing idle.
The “Create Paste” button remains disabled during the entire operation to prevent duplicate submissions, and the status panel is the primary indicator (not a delayed secondary indicator).

file_operations.description: Extend the UploadProgress model to include a pre-upload stage (e.g., “preparing”) and invoke the progress callback immediately at mutation start and before each potentially slow file-read step, so the UI can render feedback before byte-level upload progress begins. Use the blob-storage ExternalBlob progress capability already available (Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing).

Add UI handling for the new pre-upload stage (e.g., show “Preparing files…” / “Preparing file X of Y”, with an appropriate loader and/or non-advancing progress presentation) while keeping existing uploading/creating states intact.

On form submit, set uploadProgress immediately to a preparing state before awaiting the mutation (and optionally yield one frame so React can paint), ensuring the status panel is visible right away and remains visible until completion/error; keep the submit button disabled for the whole operation.

requirements.summary: Reduce perceived and actual slow upload behavior by minimizing time-to-first-feedback and time-to-first-upload-progress while keeping the 50MB per-file limit and friendly errors.
acceptanceCriteria: Time-to-first-user-feedback is improved: users see immediate status feedback on submit even before byte-level upload progress begins.
Time-to-first-upload-progress event is minimized (e.g., by restructuring the client flow to begin progress reporting earlier and avoiding unnecessary blocking work before starting the upload).
Upload still enforces the existing 50MB per-file limit and errors remain user-friendly in English.
No changes require additional backend canisters or external services; the system remains single-actor on the backend.

file_operations.description: Restructure the create-paste client flow to avoid unnecessary blocking before upload begins: emit “preparing” progress before reading each file into memory, and update progress stage transitions so users see continuous feedback from click → preparation → upload → finalization. Continue to leverage ExternalBlob.withUploadProgress for byte-level reporting during the upload (Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing).

Keep existing 50MB per-file enforcement via pasteLimits and ensure any new intermediate/preparing messages remain clear and professional in English; ensure error handling continues to show friendly messages if preparation/upload fails.
